features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Participants may borrow from their fund account a minimum of $1,000 up to a maximum equal to the lesser of 50% of the participant's vested account balance, or $50,000.
features.autoEnrollText: The Plan includes a provision for automatic enrollment at a pre‐tax deferral rate of 3%.

features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Participants may borrow from their fund accounts a minimum of $1,000 up to a maximum equal to the lesser of $50,000 or 50 percent of their vested account balance.

features.matchText: The Company may make matching contributions equal to a discretionary percentage, to be determined by the employer, of the participant's elective deferrals.
features.vestingText: Employer contributions are vested as follows: Years of Percent Service Vested 1 0 2 20 3 40 4 60 5 80 6 100 Over 6 100 E.

features.matchText: The Board of Directors elected to make a matching contribution of 25% of the participant’s deferral on the first 5% of the participant’s eligible compensation for the years ended .
features.vestingText: A participant was fully vested in discretionary matching and profit-sharing contributions after three years of credited service.

features.matchText: The Network will make fixed, tiered matching contributions equal to a uniform percentage of each tier of each participant’s elective deferrals.
features.vestingText: mployees (Meridian Cash Balance Frozen DB Plan employees) who are eligible for an additional 3% transition credit employer nonelective contribution that is calculated and contributed annually and subject to 3-year cliff vesting.
features.rothText: Participants may also designate contributions as Roth contributions.
features.eligibilityText: To be eligible for this additional employer contribution, a Meridian Cash Balance Pension Plan participant must be at least age 50 with at least 10 years of service as of .
features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Participants may borrow from their fund accounts the lesser of $50,000 or 50% of their vested account balance.

features.vestingText: For the period of January 1, 2023 through September 22, 2023 and the year ended 2022, no employer contributions were made to the Plan Vesting Participants are immediately vested 100% in all pre-tax and Roth contributions plus actual earnings thereon.
features.rothText: Contributions The Plan provides for employee pre-tax and Roth contributions.
features.eligibilityText: Eligibility Eligible employees can participate in the Plan upon hire in accordance with the Plan Document.
features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Eligible participants may borrow from their Plan accounts in amounts that can be adequately secured by the participant's vested balance, for a maximum term of two years.
features.autoEnrollText: The Plan includes an auto- enrollment provision whereby all newly eligible employees are automatically enrolled in the Plan unless they affirmatively elect not to participate in the Plan.

features.matchText: Participant Accounts Each participant’s account was credited with the participant’s contributions, Company matching contributions and allocations of Plan earnings.
features.vestingText: Vesting in the Company’s matching contribution portion of their accounts plus actual earnings thereon was based on years of service.
features.rothText: Participant salary deferrals could be traditional 401(k) (pretax) and/or Roth 401(k) (after-tax).
